chore: register ewma module in lib.rs (wa-p0yy)

@@ -1004,19 +1004,19 @@
 {"id":"wa-oacw","title":"TUI events feed: recent/unhandled, severity colors, select to view details","description":"# Task: TUI events feed

## Goal
Provide an interactive event feed that makes it trivial to:
- see **what wa thinks is happening**
- quickly spot **unhandled / high-severity** events
- drill into why a rule matched (pack/rule/trace)

This is the “incident timeline” view.

## Data contract
Back the view via the same model as `wa robot events`:
- event id
- timestamp
- pane id + domain
- severity
- handled/unhandled status
- rule/pack identifiers
- short redacted summary
- optional match trace (what text matched, in redacted form)

## UX requirements
- Feed view:
  - default sort: newest-first
  - toggle: unhandled-only
  - filter by pane id / domain / event type
  - severity coloring (must degrade gracefully for non-color terminals)

- Event details panel:
  - show full structured fields
  - show rule id + pack id
  - show remediation hint / “recommended next action” if present

## Safety
- Never render raw transcript content that could include secrets.
- Always use redacted summaries/traces.

## Testing strategy
- Unit tests (feature `tui`) using fake data:
  - filter toggles work
  - severity sorting is stable
  - details panel renders expected fields
  - redaction is preserved (no accidental display of raw secrets)

## Acceptance Criteria
- User can toggle unhandled-only and immediately see unhandled critical events.
- Selecting an event shows rule/pack ids and a redacted trace/details view.

 {"id":"wa-od8xy","title":"Phase 2: Copy 30 crate directories into frankenterm/","description":"Copy 30 WezTerm crate directories from legacy_wezterm/ into frankenterm/ using rsync (excluding .git). Create frankenterm/PROVENANCE.md documenting source commit, date, MIT license, scope, and ownership intent.

## Crates copied (30 total)
async_ossl, base91, bidi, bintree, codec, color-types, config (+ config/derive), filedescriptor, lua-api-crates/termwiz-funcs, luahelper, mux, procinfo, promise, pty, rangeset, term, termwiz, umask, vtparse, wezterm-blob-leases, wezterm-cell, wezterm-char-props, wezterm-dynamic (+ wezterm-dynamic/derive), wezterm-escape-parser, wezterm-input-types, wezterm-ssh, wezterm-surface, wezterm-uds

## Excluded from workspace
- bidi/generate/ (codegen tool)
- wezterm-char-props/codegen/ (codegen tool)

## Key detail
base91 was not in the original 29-crate plan but is required by the codec crate. Added as 30th crate.

 {"id":"wa-oegrb","title":"[EPIC] Universal mux I/O flight recorder + hybrid lexical/semantic search","description":"# [EPIC] Universal Mux I/O Flight Recorder + Hybrid Search (FrankenSQLite + Tantivy + Semantic)

## Vision
Build a **flight recorder** for FrankenTerm that captures **all text entering and leaving the mux server** in an ultra-performant append-only pipeline, then exposes fast, trustworthy, hybrid lexical/semantic retrieval for operators and agents.

This epic is intentionally broader than simple logging. It defines a full system:
1. Capture/tap points for ingress and egress text
2. Durable append-only write path under high concurrency
3. Indexing and retrieval architecture (lexical + semantic)
4. Query/API surfaces for humans and robots
5. Reliability, observability, and rollout safety

## Why this matters
- Multi-agent terminal swarms generate high-volume, high-value forensic data.
- Current observability is good for event/state workflows, but not yet a true end-to-end immutable transcript plane optimized for forensic replay and hybrid retrieval.
- A robust flight recorder increases debugging speed, incident response quality, postmortem accuracy, and model/operator feedback loops.

## Design intent
- **Performance first**: append-only, batched writes, minimal per-event overhead.
- **Correctness first**: total ordering/causality metadata sufficient for replay and audit.
- **Durability first**: resilient write path (including exploration of FrankenSQLite multi-writer and WAL self-healing capabilities).
- **Search first**: Tantivy-backed lexical retrieval plus semantic retrieval and rank fusion.
- **Operator first**: stable CLI/robot/MCP interfaces and runbooks.

## Research grounding
Implementation must be informed by close study/adaptation patterns from:
- `/dp/frankensqlite`
- `/dp/coding_agent_session_search` (cass)
- `/dp/xf`

## Non-goals (for this epic)
- Replacing all existing storage paths in one unsafe big-bang migration.
- Shipping semantic retrieval without lexical fallback and measurable quality baselines.
- Adding unbounded retention without explicit cost/retention controls.

## Deliverables
- Full bead graph (research -> architecture -> implementation -> validation -> rollout)
- Feature-gated implementation plan with rollback path
- Search quality/perf test strategy and success thresholds
- Operator docs and migration guidance

## Success criteria
- Flight recorder captures ingress+egress text with stable ordering metadata.
- Append-only writer sustains swarm-scale load without destabilizing watcher/runtime.
- Lexical and hybrid search return correct results with measurable latency/quality targets.
- End-to-end system is testable, observable, and safe to roll out incrementally.

## Acceptance criteria
- All child tracks are completed or explicitly deferred with rationale.
- Dependency graph is acyclic and implementation-ready for swarm execution.
- Every major component includes unit/integration/e2e/load tests and failure-mode coverage.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:26.151022Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:26.151022Z","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","frankensqlite","search","semantic","tantivy"]}
-{"id":"wa-oegrb.1","title":"Research track: deep source-study + adaptation plan for frankensqlite/cass/xf","description":"## Track objective
Create a rigorous, implementation-grade understanding of `/dp/frankensqlite`, `/dp/coding_agent_session_search` (cass), and `/dp/xf`, then translate that into directly actionable adaptation guidance for FrankenTerm.

## Why this track exists
Premature implementation without deep source-level study risks cargo-culting and architecture drift. This track produces the factual foundation for all subsequent tracks.

## Deliverables
- Deep-dive dossiers per source project
- Cross-project architecture extraction matrix
- Adapt-vs-rewrite decisions with risk/cost rationale
- Final ADR describing the target flight-recorder/search architecture

## Acceptance criteria
- No downstream implementation bead should require re-reading external repos to understand scope.
- All key assumptions and tradeoffs are documented with explicit reasoning and test implications.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.090904Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.090904Z","labels":["cass","flight-recorder","frankensqlite","research","xf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.090904Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"wa-oegrb.1.1","title":"Study /dp/frankensqlite deeply for append-only recorder suitability","description":"## Goal
Perform a code-level architectural study of `/dp/frankensqlite` focused on applicability to flight-recorder ingestion at swarm scale.

## Background
We need evidence-based decisions about adopting FrankenSQLite capabilities (multi-writer concurrency, WAL durability model, self-healing behaviors, operational characteristics) instead of speculative integration.

## Scope
- Map write path internals relevant to append-only transcript ingest.
- Identify integration seams (API boundaries, transaction model, backpressure behavior, checkpointing).
- Characterize durability and recovery semantics, including WAL fault scenarios.
- Document required adapter layer in FrankenTerm.

## Deliverables
- Self-contained technical dossier with architecture diagrams, hot-path notes, risks, and recommended integration patterns.
- "Adopt as-is vs adapt vs avoid" table for key FrankenSQLite subsystems.

## Acceptance criteria
- Dossier includes concrete code references and explicit assumptions.
- At least one proposed integration path and one fallback path are described.
- Open risks are converted into explicit downstream beads.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.499014Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:11:06.125639Z","labels":["flight-recorder","frankensqlite","research"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.1.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.499014Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}]}

Includes append-log suitability analysis, durability/recovery behavior, backpressure primitives, and copy/adapt/avoid integration guidance for recorder tracks.","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:06:09Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.1.2","title":"Study /dp/coding_agent_session_search (cass) two-tier search architecture","description":"## Goal
Perform a deep source study of `/dp/coding_agent_session_search` (cass), emphasizing its two-tier architecture (raw store + search layer) and hybrid lexical/semantic patterns.

## Background
cass is directly relevant to our target capability. We need precise extraction of what to copy/adapt and what to re-implement for FrankenTerm constraints.

## Scope
- Map ingest pipeline and data normalization strategy.
- Map Tantivy schema/analyzers, query semantics, and ranking behavior.
- Map semantic retrieval components and fusion strategy.
- Identify assumptions that do not hold for live mux flight-recorder data.

## Deliverables
- Self-contained cass architecture dossier.
- Candidate reusable modules/patterns list with compatibility score.

## Acceptance criteria
- Dossier includes direct mapping from cass concepts to FrankenTerm components.
- Explicit list of "copy/adapt/avoid" decisions with rationale.","notes":"Completed deep source study of cass two-tier architecture. Added dossier at docs/flight-recorder/cass-two-tier-architecture-dossier.md with: ingest/storage/search mapping, lexical/semantic/hybrid flow, assumptions mismatch for live mux data, and explicit copy/adapt/avoid decisions + compatibility scores.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.532976Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:22:23.863782Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T06:22:23.863735Z","labels":["cass","research","search","semantic","tantivy"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.1.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.532976Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.1.3","title":"Study /dp/xf deeply for scalable hybrid retrieval design patterns","description":"## Goal
Perform a deep source study of `/dp/xf` with focus on high-volume indexing/search, hybrid retrieval behavior, and production-operational search patterns.

## Background
xf provides a second reference implementation with potentially different tradeoffs than cass. We need both to triangulate a robust design.

## Scope
- Map data pipeline stages and indexing strategy.
- Map lexical and semantic retrieval plumbing.
- Map ranking logic, query APIs, and performance controls.
- Extract reliability and operational patterns relevant to FrankenTerm.

## Deliverables
- Self-contained xf architecture dossier.
- Delta analysis vs cass (what xf does better/worse for our use-case).

## Acceptance criteria
- Dossier clearly identifies portable patterns and non-portable assumptions.
- Cross-project comparison hooks are prepared for the synthesis bead.","notes":"Completed deep xf source-study and added dossier artifact at docs/flight-recorder/xf-hybrid-retrieval-dossier.md with architecture mapping, portability analysis, and detailed delta-vs-cass synthesis hooks for wa-oegrb.1.4.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverHarbor","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.565787Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:29:57.327657Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T06:29:57.327586Z","labels":["hybrid","research","search","xf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.1.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.565787Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"wa-oegrb.1.4","title":"Build cross-project extraction matrix (frankensqlite vs cass vs xf)","description":"## Goal
Synthesize findings from frankensqlite/cass/xf into a single adaptation matrix that directly drives implementation sequencing.

## Background
Separate studies are not enough; we need a unified decision artifact that translates research into implementation-ready work packages.

## Scope
- Build a matrix by subsystem: ingest/write durability/indexing/query/ranking/ops.
- For each subsystem: identify source project precedent, adaptation cost, risk, and test implications.
- Produce explicit recommendations for FrankenTerm target architecture.

## Deliverables
- Architecture extraction matrix (self-contained and implementation-oriented).
- Traceability table linking each recommendation to downstream beads.

## Acceptance criteria
- Matrix includes risk/complexity scores and dependency hints.
- Downstream track owners can implement without revisiting source repos.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.598965Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.75291Z","labels":["architecture","planning","research"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.1.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.598965Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.1.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.6938Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.1.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.723532Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.1.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.752861Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"wa-oegrb.1.5","title":"Author ADR: target flight-recorder architecture + phased implementation map","description":"## Goal
Write an ADR for the target flight-recorder + hybrid-search architecture and lock first implementation increment boundaries.

## Background
A formal decision record reduces churn and prevents drift once implementation starts across multiple agents.

## Scope
- Define canonical architecture for v1 rollout.
- Define phased implementation sequence (MVP -> hardened -> scale).
- Define explicit non-goals, deferrals, and rollback boundaries.
- Define success metrics and gate criteria per phase.

## Deliverables
- ADR with decision rationale and alternatives considered.
- Sequenced execution map and handoff checklist for implementation agents.

## Acceptance criteria
- ADR is self-contained and references only stable bead artifacts.
- All implementation tracks have explicit entry conditions and dependencies.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.631865Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.782204Z","labels":["adr","architecture","planning"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.1.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.631865Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.1.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.782153Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"wa-oegrb.1.6","title":"Run feasibility spikes for writer/indexer/hybrid rank hot paths","description":"## Goal
Execute focused technical spikes that validate the highest-risk assumptions before full implementation begins.

## Background
Research and ADR reduce uncertainty, but spikes are needed to validate hot-path feasibility and integration complexity.

## Scope
- Spike append-only write throughput envelope with representative event volume.
- Spike Tantivy incremental indexing from append log snapshots.
- Spike hybrid query fusion behavior on a small truth-set.

## Deliverables
- Spike report with measured metrics and pass/fail against target budgets.
- Updated risk register and recommended sequencing adjustments.

## Acceptance criteria
- At least one spike per risk category (write path, indexing, ranking).
- Failures convert into explicit blocker beads before implementation proceeds.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.663951Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.81142Z","labels":["performance","search","spike"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.1.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.663951Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.1.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:03:44.811381Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"wa-oegrb.2","title":"Capture track: canonical ingress/egress event schema + mux tap semantics","description":"## Track objective
Define and implement the canonical flight-recorder event model and capture tap points for **all mux ingress/egress text** with deterministic ordering metadata.

## Why this track exists
If capture semantics are ambiguous, durability and search become untrustworthy. This track establishes the contract for what is captured, in what order, and with what context.

## Deliverables
- Event schema (ingress/egress/control markers/metadata)
- Tap-point implementation plan for mux boundaries
- Causality/sequence model across panes and workflows
- Backpressure/failure semantics for capture stage

## Acceptance criteria
- Event schema is versioned, testable, and replay-compatible.
- Capture contract is explicit enough for independent indexing/search teams to implement against it.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.18975Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.645175Z","labels":["capture","flight-recorder","schema"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.18975Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.645129Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.2.1","title":"Define versioned recorder event schema for ingress/egress mux text","description":"## Goal
Define a versioned canonical event schema for mux ingress/egress text capture, including metadata needed for replay, search filtering, and causality analysis.

## Background
Schema ambiguity will invalidate downstream indexing and replay. This bead establishes the ground-truth contract.

## Scope
- Define event types (ingress text, egress output, control markers, lifecycle markers).
- Define required metadata (pane/session/workflow correlation IDs, timestamps, sequence numbers, source).
- Define schema evolution/versioning rules and compatibility tests.

## Deliverables
- Recorder event schema spec and serialization contract.
- JSON-schema or equivalent testable schema artifact.

## Acceptance criteria
- Schema is precise enough for independent producer/consumer implementation.
- Evolution rules cover additive and breaking changes explicitly.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkGate","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.66701Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T02:11:47.023622-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-12T02:11:47.023622-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented alongside ft-oegrb.2.1 — same deliverables.","labels":["capture","flight-recorder","schema"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.66701Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.2.2","title":"Implement ingress tap points for all mux-bound text/action injections","description":"## Goal
Implement ingress capture tap points (text/actions entering mux/panes) with minimal overhead and explicit source attribution.

## Background
Ingress visibility is required for full forensic replay and for understanding operator/agent actions leading to outcomes.

## Scope
- Identify ingress boundaries and hook locations.
- Capture payload + metadata in canonical schema.
- Ensure capture path does not break policy gating behavior.

## Deliverables
- Ingress capture implementation plan + instrumentation tests.
- Validation that policy decisions and ingress logs remain consistent.

## Acceptance criteria
- Ingress events are captured for all supported injection paths.
- Overhead budget for ingress capture is documented and met in benchmarks.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.699328Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.864573Z","labels":["capture","ingress","mux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.699328Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.864528Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.2.3","title":"Implement egress tap points with contiguous segment + explicit gap semantics","description":"## Goal
Implement egress capture tap points (pane/mux output leaving runtime paths) with contiguous segment semantics and explicit gap markers.

## Background
Egress capture is the primary data source for replay and search; it must preserve ordering and discontinuity semantics.

## Scope
- Hook egress at the authoritative output pipeline.
- Emit explicit discontinuity markers where continuity cannot be guaranteed.
- Preserve compatibility with existing ingest/pattern detection semantics.

## Deliverables
- Egress capture instrumentation with segment/gap semantics.
- Regression tests for continuity/gap behavior.

## Acceptance criteria
- Egress events are complete for observed panes.
- Gap semantics are explicit and machine-detectable.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.731497Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.894337Z","labels":["capture","egress","ingest"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.731497Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.894294Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.2.4","title":"Define deterministic sequence + correlation model for replayable causality","description":"## Goal
Define deterministic sequence and correlation rules across ingress/egress streams so replay/search can reconstruct causality.

## Background
Raw timestamps alone are insufficient under concurrency. We need deterministic ordering semantics and correlation keys.

## Scope
- Define sequence assignment rules per pane and global merge strategy.
- Define correlation metadata (pane UUID/session/workflow/action linkage).
- Define clock-skew and race-handling behavior.

## Deliverables
- Ordering/correlation specification.
- Determinism and replay correctness test plan.

## Acceptance criteria
- Ordering rules are unambiguous and test-assertable.
- Replay results are stable across repeated runs on same input.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.764919Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.955988Z","labels":["capture","ordering","replay"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.764919Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.925809Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.955931Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"wa-oegrb.2.5","title":"Define capture-stage redaction policy and deterministic implementation boundaries","description":"## Goal
Specify and implement capture-stage redaction boundaries so sensitive tokens are protected without destroying search/replay utility.

## Background
A full recorder introduces privacy/security risk. Redaction policy must be explicit early, not bolted on later.

## Scope
- Define sensitive-pattern classes and redaction modes.
- Define where redaction is applied (before persist, before index, before query response).
- Define auditability for redaction actions.

## Deliverables
- Redaction policy and implementation requirements.
- Tests for deterministic redaction behavior and false-positive guardrails.

## Acceptance criteria
- Sensitive fields are consistently protected across storage/index/API layers.
- Redaction behavior is measurable and tunable.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.797785Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.987589Z","labels":["capture","privacy","redaction","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.797785Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.987531Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"wa-oegrb.2.6","title":"Specify and validate capture backpressure/overflow behavior under swarm load","description":"## Goal
Define capture-path backpressure/failure semantics and verify behavior under overload so recorder does not destabilize core watcher operations.

## Background
Recorder capture must fail safely under stress; silent degradation is unacceptable.

## Scope
- Define queueing and overflow policy (drop/slow/spool) with rationale.
- Define telemetry and alerting conditions.
- Validate with overload and fault injection tests.

## Deliverables
- Backpressure policy doc and implementation plan.
- Overload test scenarios with expected outcomes.

## Acceptance criteria
- Overload behavior is deterministic and observable.
- Core watcher stability is preserved under recorder stress.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.831967Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:12.048598Z","labels":["backpressure","capture","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:11.831967Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:12.018117Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.2.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:04:12.048557Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.3","title":"Storage track: append-only writer path + backend abstraction + FrankenSQLite integration","description":"## Track objective
Build the append-only persistence path for flight-recorder events, including backend abstraction, high-throughput writer path, and FrankenSQLite integration path with safe fallback.

## Why this track exists
Logging quality is bounded by write-path reliability and throughput. This track ensures durability under load while preserving rollout safety.

## Deliverables
- Storage abstraction for recorder log backends
- Baseline backend (for immediate bring-up)
- FrankenSQLite backend plan/integration path
- Crash recovery/replay and corruption handling strategy

## Acceptance criteria
- Sustained append throughput targets are met under concurrent writer load.
- Failure modes are explicit and exercised in automated tests.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.285668Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.717242Z","labels":["append-only","flight-recorder","frankensqlite","storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.285668Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.678028Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.717172Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.3.1","title":"Define recorder storage abstraction and backend-gated configuration","description":"## Context
The recorder must support multiple storage backends during rollout, including an immediate low-risk path and an advanced FrankenSQLite-backed path. We need a stable contract before implementation to avoid coupling capture, storage internals, and query/index pipelines.

## Scope
Define a versioned `RecorderStorage` abstraction (append, flush, checkpoint/read cursor, health, lag metrics) plus config/feature-gate surfaces selecting backend at runtime.

## Deliverables
- Trait/interface boundary and data contracts (event envelope + batch semantics)
- Backend selection config model with explicit default/fallback behavior
- Failure taxonomy and retry contract (retryable vs terminal)
- ADR notes on why this interface is shaped for high-throughput append-only ingestion

## Why this matters
A narrow, explicit storage contract prevents premature lock-in and lets us ship incrementally without rewriting capture or query layers.

## Acceptance
- Interface is sufficient for both local append-log backend and FrankenSQLite backend
- Error and durability semantics are explicit and testable
- Feature flags/config behavior are documented and deterministic","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:49.965804Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.659049Z","labels":["architecture","epic","flight-recorder","track-storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:49.965804Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.618035Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.658992Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
